top_stories.title: Chinese hackers hijack auth flow, spy on isolated network for a decade
top_stories.summary: Chinese hackers hijacked an organization's authentication infrastructure and maintained persistent access for approximately 10 years. The attackers gained full visibility into administrative activity by taking control of the authentication stack, representing a long-term, deep compromise of identity and access controls.

top_stories.title: CISA orders feds to patch actively exploited Ivanti flaw by Sunday
top_stories.summary: CISA issued Binding Operational Directive BOD 26-04 ordering federal agencies to patch an actively exploited Ivanti Sentry vulnerability within three days. Ivanti Sentry is an access management gateway product, making this directly relevant to IAM infrastructure security.

top_stories.title: Oracle mitigates PeopleSoft zero-day exploited in data theft attacks
top_stories.summary: Oracle has disclosed a critical zero-day vulnerability (CVE-2026-35273) in PeopleSoft Suite that enables unauthenticated remote code execution and has been actively exploited by the ShinyHunters threat actor in data theft attacks. Oracle has issued a mitigation for the flaw, which is particularly relevant to IAM professionals as PeopleSoft includes HR and identity-related data and integrates with enterprise identity systems.

top_stories.title: Researchers release details, PoC for exploited Check Point VPN flaw (CVE-2026-50751)
top_stories.summary: WatchTowr researchers published a technical analysis and proof-of-concept "Detection Artefact Generator" for CVE-2026-50751, an authentication bypass vulnerability in Check Point's Remote Access VPN and Mobile Access software. The flaw was patched by Check Point on June 8, 2026, and had already been confirmed as actively exploited in limited attacks prior to public disclosure.

top_stories.title: China-Linked Hackers Backdoored Linux Login Software to Hide for Nearly a Decade
top_stories.summary: A China-linked threat group tracked as Velvet Ant backdoored Linux PAM (Pluggable Authentication Modules) and OpenSSH components to maintain persistent unauthorized access for nearly a decade. The attackers targeted the authentication layer itself, embedding malicious code in the systems that control login access, making detection and remediation through standard security cleanup ineffective.

news.title: Ex-school district employee jailed for hacks on former employer
news.summary: A former IT employee at an Iowa school district was sentenced to 21 months in prison for conducting cyberattacks against his former employer. The attacks involved deleting accounts and disrupting systems, causing tens of thousands of dollars in damages.

news.title: phpBB forum fixes auth bypass bug lurking for a decade
news.summary: phpBB forum software has patched a 10-year-old authentication bypass vulnerability that allowed attackers to log in as any user, including administrators. The flaw represents a critical authentication control failure affecting account takeover scenarios across installations of the widely-used open-source forum platform.

news.title: How to use NIST and ISO frameworks to govern AI agents
news.summary: The article outlines how NIST and ISO frameworks can be applied to govern AI agents operating autonomously in enterprise environments. Key IAM-relevant concerns include AI agents carrying delegated authority, invoking internal APIs, and making decisions that traditionally require human oversight — directly touching on authorization and identity governance principles.

news.title: ShinyHunters Exploits Oracle PeopleSoft Zero-Day (CVE-2026-35273) to Breach Universities
news.summary: The ShinyHunters threat group exploited a zero-day vulnerability in Oracle PeopleSoft (CVE-2026-35273) to breach enterprise systems, primarily targeting universities, between May 27 and June 9. Oracle did not publish its advisory until June 10, meaning the flaw was unpatched during the attack window, with Mandiant attributing the activity to a cluster tracked as UNC6240.

news.title: Linx Security Integrates with Claude Compliance API to Bring Identity Governance to Enterprise AI
news.summary: Linx Security announced an integration between its identity security and governance platform and the Claude Compliance API. The integration is designed to extend identity governance capabilities to enterprise AI environments, covering human, AI agent, and non-human identities.

news.title: Microsoft Entra adds device soft-delete and cloud-native sync migration
news.summary: Microsoft Entra has introduced a soft-delete feature for device objects in public preview, enabling administrators to recover accidentally deleted Entra joined or registered devices within a retention period. The update also includes a cloud-native sync migration capability, with the soft-delete mechanism preserving critical identity and configuration data associated with managed devices.

news.title: Levi & Korsinsky Announces Investigation of Securities Claims Against SailPoint, Inc. (SAIL)
news.summary: Law firm Levi & Korsinsky has announced a securities fraud investigation into SailPoint, Inc. (SAIL) following a significant stock drop of approximately 12% after a negative forward outlook was disclosed on June 9, 2026. The investigation centers on whether SailPoint misled investors about its growth prospects prior to the disclosure.

news.title: Teleport Named Most Innovative Identity and Access Management Platform in 2026 Cybersecurity Stars Awards
news.summary: Teleport has been named the most innovative Identity and Access Management Platform in The Hacker News 2026 Cybersecurity Stars Awards. The recognition focuses on Teleport's infrastructure identity architecture, which is designed to deliver resiliency, scalability, and agility to modern computing environments.

news.title: IDMExpress Announces 24×7 Managed Services for Leading IAM/PAM Solutions with Innovative Unified Pricing Model
news.summary: IDMExpress launched a 24×7 Managed Identity Security Operations service covering IAM, PAM, and PKI platforms with a unified pricing model. The service offers a single bucket of hours usable across any supported platform with no vendor lock-in, based in Clark, NJ.

news.title: Enterprise Identities Are Under Siege. Josys Is Changing How Organizations Fight Back.
news.summary: Josys has announced new AI-native platform capabilities targeting enterprise identity management challenges. The features are designed to address credential theft, AI agent governance, and SaaS access policy gaps.

news.title: Not all AIs are equal so CIOs need to prioritize actions when assessing risks
news.summary: The article addresses how CIOs should approach identity governance and risk-based security controls as AI agents proliferate. The piece focuses on prioritizing actions when assessing AI-related risks, with identity governance cited as a key consideration.

news.title: Threat actors are recruiting the people who hold cloud logins
news.summary: Threat actors are actively recruiting insiders and targeting employees who hold cloud platform credentials to gain unauthorized access to corporate data and applications. The attack methodology exploits the credential-based access model of cloud platforms, making identity and access management a central vulnerability in enterprise security postures.

news.title: ServiceNow Flaw Exploited to Gain Unauthorized Access to Customer Instances
news.summary: ServiceNow applied a security update on June 5, 2026, after threat actors exploited a vulnerability to gain unauthorized access to customer instances. The flaw allowed unauthenticated users to obtain deeper access to susceptible hosted instances, with the company issuing an advisory to affected customers.

news.title: Acsense Achieves FedRAMP Certification, Extending IAM Resilience to Federal Agencies
news.summary: Acsense has achieved FedRAMP certification, making its IAM resilience platform available to federal agencies through Knox Systems. The certification covers purpose-built protection, recovery, and continuous compliance validation for Okta and Microsoft Entra ID environments.

news.title: Netwrix 2026 Data and Identity Security Report: AI Adoption Outpacing AI Readiness, Driving a 4x Breach Gap
news.summary: Netwrix released its 2026 Data and Identity Security Report, finding that AI adoption is outpacing security readiness and creating a significant breach gap among organizations. Key findings include that only 11% of organizations report full AI security readiness, 17% remain entirely unprepared, and 45% are still developing governance programs.

news.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
news.summary: Hackers successfully hijacked high-profile Instagram accounts by manipulating Meta's AI support chatbot into resetting account passwords. Instructions for the social engineering technique were shared on Telegram, enabling attackers to bypass normal account authentication controls to seize accounts belonging to the Obama White House and a U.S. Space Force official.

news.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
news.summary: A CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and other agency secrets to a public GitHub account, causing a data breach that CISA is still working to contain. Congressional lawmakers from both chambers are demanding answers while CISA works to invalidate the leaked credentials.

news.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
news.summary: A CISA contractor exposed credentials for highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and numerous internal CISA systems in a public GitHub repository. The leaked files reportedly detailed CISA's internal software build, test, and deployment processes, representing a significant privileged credential exposure incident.
